description: The switcher's trailing muted plus circle; tapping it appends a new blank counter, the tap equivalent of swiping past the last counter.

description: Observable store for system-wide app defaults (handedness, sound-on-change choice, and two directional haptic-on-change choices) persisted in UserDefaults. Handedness coerces via bool(forKey:); the SoundOption/HapticOption enums decode their rawValue from string(forKey:) through HapticOption.resolve so removed amplitude/rigid values migrate to a surviving feel. soundOption falls back to .off; incrementHapticOption/decrementHapticOption default to a distinct pairing (increment .sharp, decrement .soft) and migrate a legacy single hapticOption key into both directions when the new keys are absent. The single default source per-counter overrides resolve against.
name: AppSettings

description: The system-wide App Settings panel. Holds no local edit state: each control writes straight through to the persisted default, so there is no save or cancel.
name: AppSettingsPanel

untestabilityReason.justification: The Android target has no isolated-component harness: Paparazzi is not configured and `isolate` scaffolds previews into a foreign package, so no component scenario can render this composable standalone. Its states are shown by the `android-counter-app-settings-open` application scenario. Remove this reason once an Android isolation harness exists and a componentName-matched scenario is captured.

description: Floating overlay panel for system-wide App Settings, bound to AppSettings. Holds a HANDEDNESS segmented control, SOUND and HAPTIC option pickers (horizontally-scrolling pills that auto-scroll the active choice into view), and an ALL COUNTERS button; mirrors CounterSettingsPanel chrome (DONE to close). Each control writes straight through to the persisted default.
name: AppSettingsPanel

description: The lower row of RESET / SUBTRACT / GRAPH controls plus the increment button's downward extension, mirrored as a whole by handedness.

description: A single named tally (id, name, count, colorKey, allowNegative, step, order) plus optional per-counter overrides of the app-wide defaults: handednessOverride (Bool?), soundOverride (SoundOption?), and two directional haptics incrementHapticOverride/decrementHapticOverride (HapticOption?). A nil override follows AppSettings; a set value pins it. Carries the effectiveLeftHanded/effectiveSound/effectiveIncrementHaptic/effectiveDecrementHaptic resolvers that return the override when present else the supplied app default, and on decode migrates a legacy single 'hapticOverride' into both directions and decodes unrecognized override values as nil so older persisted counters load unchanged.

untestabilityReason.justification: Protocol declaration; behavior is exercised through its implementations' tests in FeedbackTests.

description: One run of a counter's activity between resets: a startedAt plus an ordered list of CounterEvents. Provides pure helpers for the cumulative running-count series and each event's relative offset from the start; persisted per-counter under the counterHistories key, capped at 10.
name: CounterHistory

description: The sliders and chart glyphs the core Material icon set does not carry, hand-built as small vectors rather than pulling in material-icons-extended for two icons.

description: Scrollable overlay listing every counter (colored dot + name + count), opened from App Settings. Tapping a row selects that counter and dismisses the list; blank slots render with the muted dot and em-dash placeholder. The active counter's row carries the accent ring.
name: CounterListPanel

description: Observable store backing the counter screen. Loads seeded or default counters and the selected id from UserDefaults at launch, tracks the active selection, and applies increment, subtract with optional clamping when negatives are disallowed, reset, and wrap-around swipe selection, persisting every change back to UserDefaults. On each count change it emits feedback resolved through the active counter's effective sound and per-direction haptic overrides via the effectiveFeedback closure, picking the increment vs decrement haptic by direction before calling the feedback sink; updateActiveCounter round-trips the per-counter handedness/sound and both directional haptic overrides. deleteCounter is keyed on position via baseSlotCount: the first four positions are permanent base slots that are blanked in place so the row never shrinks below them, while a counter added beyond them is removed outright and the previous counter becomes active, dropping its recorded runs.
name: CounterModel

description: The Android counter screen: header, switcher card, count hero and bottom control assembly, plus the swipe-to-switch gesture and the four floating overlays. The Compose port of iOS ContentView.
name: CounterScreen

description: The Compose state layer over the framework-free CounterModel and AppSettings. Routes every mutation through a revision counter so the in-place-mutating model drives recomposition, and owns the four overlay flags (seeded only from a trusted store).
name: CounterScreenState

description: The per-counter settings panel expanding over the count: name, colour, step, allow-negative, the collapsible feedback overrides and Delete. Local state holds in-progress edits; DONE saves.
name: CounterSettingsPanel

untestabilityReason.justification: The Android target has no isolated-component harness: Paparazzi is not configured and `isolate` scaffolds previews into a foreign package, so no component scenario can render this composable standalone. description: Inline settings panel that expands over the count numeral. description: The destructive delete control in the counter settings panel, guarded by a two-tap confirm. Disarmed it is an outlined coffee-colored DELETE COUNTER; the first tap arms it into a filled TAP AGAIN TO CONFIRM that auto-disarms after about 3s, so an accidental single tap never wipes a counter, and a second tap while armed invokes onDelete. The whole outlined frame is the tap target via contentShape, because the disarmed button's clear background is not hit-testable on its own. A confirming init parameter seeds the armed state so a static scenario can capture it without a live tap.

description: Static migration helper on HapticOption that resolves a persisted rawValue - a per-direction key, the legacy single key, or a Counter override - into a current case, mapping the removed amplitude values rigid/heavy/medium to sharp and light to soft, and returning nil for a genuinely unknown token so callers fall back to a default.

description: Shared press styling for the two faces of the increment button: dims its label from a shared pressed binding and syncs its own isPressed back into that binding, so pressing either face dims both and the L-shape reads as one surface. An explicit .animation keyed on the shared pressed value pins both faces to one identical dim curve — without it the touched face rides SwiftUI's implicit press transaction while its partner snaps, and the L visibly splits in two mid-press.
name: IncrementFaceButtonStyle

description: The default CounterFeedback: does nothing, keeping unit tests, SwiftUI previews, and the macOS build silent and deterministic.

Generic over any Hashable option; the chip row scrolls horizontally for the wider SOUND and HAPTIC lists. Backs the panel's HANDEDNESS, SOUND, and HAPTIC override rows.
name: OverridePicker

description: The pinned title plus DONE action row shared by all three panels.
name: PanelHeader

untestabilityReason.justification: A Row of two fixed Texts with a click handler on the second — no conditional rendering, iteration, or derived state.

description: A point in chart plot space. A plain holder so the geometry carries no Compose type.
name: PlotPoint

description: The chart's plot area inside the gutters that hold the axis labels and titles.
name: PlotRect

description: The result of mapping a cumulative series into a plot rect: the plotted points, the zero-line y, and the data-derived count/time domain the axes label against.
name: PlotResult

description: Build-configuration-aware decision for whether the app trusts UserDefaults state present at launch. Debug builds use .trustInjected so codeyam scenario seed data is adopted; distribution builds use .requireProvenance and honor store state only once the apps own provenance marker (counterStoreProvenance) is present. trustsStore reads that gate and stampProvenance writes the marker whenever the app itself persists, so stray injected or stale scenario keys are ignored in a Release build while a real users own data survives.
name: SeedPolicy

description: A labeled section in a settings panel: a small mono caption above its content.
name: SettingsField

untestabilityReason.justification: A FieldCaption above a content slot in a Column — no conditionals, no iteration, no state of its own.

description: A labeled section in the settings panel: a small mono caption above its forwarded content, used by the name field and color picker for consistent headings.
name: SettingsField

untestabilityReason.justification: Presentation-only layout wrapper: a caption Text above forwarded content, no branching or iteration.

description: The open/closed state of the two settings overlays, which are mutually exclusive: only one settings panel may be on screen at a time. Modeled as one value rather than two independent booleans so the never-both-open rule lives in one testable place instead of being re-derived at each tap site. Only the toggles enforce exclusivity; closing a panel never reopens the other.
name: SettingsOverlays

description: The shared panel chrome: pinned title and DONE row above a body that scrolls only once it outgrows the available height. The cap sits on the scroll, not the card, so a short panel hugs its content.
name: SettingsPanelCard

untestabilityReason.justification: Carries real layout logic (the available-height-derived scroll cap) that only manifests when rendered, but the Android target has no isolated-component route to drive a composable against — component scenarios exist only for the iOS target. Covered indirectly by the three panel application scenarios.

description: A labeled on/off row with the switch pinned right, used for ALLOW NEGATIVE.
name: SettingsToggleRow

untestabilityReason.justification: The Android target has no isolated-component harness: Paparazzi is not configured and `isolate` scaffolds previews into a foreign package, so no component scenario can render this composable standalone. Its states are shown by the `android-counter-counter-settings-open` application scenario. Remove this reason once an Android isolation harness exists and a componentName-matched scenario is captured.

description: A labeled on/off row in a settings panel: a monospaced label on the left, the switch pinned right. Used for ALLOW NEGATIVE. Its trailing inset is load-bearing rather than decorative — iOS draws the switch knob slightly past the control's layout bounds, so a switch sitting flush against the enclosing scroll region's edge gets its knob sliced by the scroll's clip; the inset keeps it clear of that boundary.
name: SettingsToggleRow

description: Production CounterFeedback. Owns the option gating (a non-off haptic fires the haptic emitter; a non-off sound fires the sound emitter with its choice) with injectable emitters so the gating is unit-tested. The real defaultHaptic maps each qualitatively-distinct HapticOption: soft and sharp are UIImpactFeedbackGenerator soft/rigid taps fired at full intensity, while double and buzz are UINotificationFeedbackGenerator success/error patterns; defaultSound plays a distinct AudioServicesPlaySystemSound per choice. Both passthroughs sit behind a canImport(UIKit) guard.
name: SystemCounterFeedback

description: Maps an already-formatted numeral to the hero's font size, stepping down as the string grows. Stands in for iOS's minimumScaleFactor, which Compose has no equivalent for.
name: autoSizedFontSize

description: Resolves a counter colorKey to its Compose dot color via the shared CounterTheme hex mapping.
name: dotColor

untestabilityReason.justification: A one-line adapter over CounterTheme.dotHex, whose key-to-hex mapping (including the unknown-key fallback) is already covered by CounterThemeTest.

description: Renders a count with locale digit grouping, matching SwiftUI's LocalizedStringKey interpolation so Android and iOS agree on four-digit-and-up values.
name: formatCount

description: Renders a signed event delta for the graph's event list, grouped the same way as formatCount.
name: formatDelta
